theory.canonical_statement: tion} The spatial component corresponds to position states $\ket{i}$ for $i = 1, \ldots, 153600$ associated with each DAANSphere address. The orbital angular momentum component spans states $\ket{m}$ for integer $m \in [-m_{\max}, m_{\max}]$ corresponding to Laguerre-Gaussian mode indices. The polarization component distinguishes horizontal and vertical polarization states $\ket{H}$ and $\ket{V}$. The frequency component describes the continuous spectrum of electromagnetic field oscillations. Pure quantum states correspond to normalized vectors $\ket{\psi} \in \mathcal{H}_{\text{total}}$ with $\braket{\psi|\psi} = 1$. Mixed states require density operator formalism, where states are represented by Hermitian positive semidefinite operators $\rho$ with unit trace satisfying $\rho = \rho^\dagger$, $\rho \geq 0$, and $\text{Tr}(\rho) = 1$. \subsection{Quantum Channel Formalism and CPTP Maps} Evolution through the rotating helix optical system corresponds to a quantum channel, mathematically described by a completely positive trace-preserving linear map. \begin{definition}[Helical Quantum Channel] The propagatio
theory.technical_interpretation: Definition of braketψ|ψ is an algebraic definition, written braketψ|ψ = 1. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
